title: Traffic Offenses - Farris Law Firm
metaDescription: The California Vehicle Code contains dozens of potential violations that could result in a citation and the dreaded obligation of showing up to traffic
contentHtml:
The California Vehicle Code contains dozens of potential violations that could result in a citation and the dreaded obligation of showing up to traffic court. Speeding, stop sign violations, hit-and-run, driving with a suspended license, expired registration, failure to show proof of insurance, driving the wrong way, cell phone use while driving, improper window tint – these are a small selection of potential traffic violations.

While traffic citations result in fines, not jail, this is a burden that we can help you handle. Farris Law Firm has extensive traffic citation knowledge, an excellent record of getting cases dismissed or reduced, and will make every effort to avoid high fines and points on your license.

How Can Farris Law Firm Help?

DON’T just pay your traffic ticket! This is admitting guilt and can result in higher insurance premiums and points on your license! 

The citation may not be filed with the court until 30 days after the traffic stop – Farris Law Firm will monitor the filing for you, attend the multiple required court dates in traffic court (including trial), and negotiate with the judge for reductions, dismissals, and traffic school to avoid high penalties.

Old traffic citations can cause major problems, too – they can be sent to collections and even prevent you from renewing your license, putting you at risk for further citations if you keep driving. If one of your charges is a misdemeanor, missing court will result in a bench warrant. Contact the team at Farris Law Firm immediately if you get a traffic citation.
